name: SOC 2 Type II — Trust Services Criteria
description: AICPA Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy. The Security category (CC) is mandatory; others are selected based on applicable trust service categories.

controls.title: COSO Principle 1 — Integrity and Ethical Values
controls.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.

controls.title: COSO Principle 2 — Board Independence and Oversight
controls.description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.

controls.title: COSO Principle 3 — Organizational Structure and Accountability
controls.description: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.

controls.title: COSO Principle 4 — Commitment to Competence
controls.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.

controls.title: COSO Principle 5 — Accountability
controls.description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.

controls.title: COSO Principle 13 — Uses Relevant Information
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.

controls.title: COSO Principle 14 — Internal Communication
controls.description: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.

controls.title: COSO Principle 15 — External Communication
controls.description: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.

controls.title: COSO Principle 6 — Specifies Suitable Objectives
controls.description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.

controls.title: COSO Principle 7 — Identifies and Analyzes Risk
controls.description: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how the risks should be managed.

controls.title: COSO Principle 8 — Assesses Fraud Risk
controls.description: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.

controls.title: COSO Principle 9 — Identifies and Analyzes Significant Change
controls.description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.

controls.title: COSO Principle 16 — Conducts Ongoing or Separate Evaluations
controls.description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.

controls.title: COSO Principle 17 — Evaluates and Communicates Deficiencies
controls.description: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action.

controls.title: COSO Principle 10 — Selects and Develops Control Activities
controls.description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.

controls.title: COSO Principle 11 — Selects and Develops General Controls Over Technology
controls.description: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.

controls.title: COSO Principle 12 — Deploys Through Policies and Procedures
controls.description: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.

controls.title: Logical Access Security Measures
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events.

controls.title: Access Provisioning and Removal
controls.description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.

controls.title: Role-Based Access and Least Privilege
controls.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on approved, documented requests.

controls.title: Physical Access Restrictions
controls.description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to authorized personnel.

controls.title: Logical and Physical Protections Against Threats
controls.description: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished.

controls.title: Threats from Outside System Boundaries
controls.description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.

controls.title: Transmission and Movement of Information
controls.description: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes.

controls.title: Prevention and Detection of Unauthorized Software
controls.description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.

controls.title: Vulnerability and Configuration Management
controls.description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that introduce new vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Monitoring for Anomalies and Security Events
controls.description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors.

controls.title: Evaluation of Security Events
controls.description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives.

controls.title: Incident Response
controls.description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program.

controls.title: Recovery from Identified Security Incidents
controls.description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.

controls.title: Change Management Process
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures.

controls.title: Risk Mitigation Activities
controls.description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.

controls.title: Vendor and Business Partner Risk Management
controls.description: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.

controls.title: Capacity Planning
controls.description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand.

controls.title: Environmental Protections
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes.

controls.title: Recovery Plan Testing
controls.description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system availability to meet its objectives.

controls.title: Identification and Classification of Confidential Information
controls.description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.

controls.title: Disposal of Confidential Information
controls.description: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.

controls.title: Complete, Accurate, and Timely Processing
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing.

controls.title: System Inputs Completely Processed
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness and accuracy.

controls.title: System Processing Complete and Accurate
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to result in products, services, and reporting.

controls.title: System Outputs Complete, Accurate, and Distributed
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely.

controls.title: Stored Items Complete and Accurate
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely and accurately.
